WELCOME_DESC: Easily control and monitor your roller shutters, gates, garage doors, or any other device using the RTS protocol.
WELCOME_RAD_DESC: Configure the correct frequency for your devices
WELCOME_SYS_DESC: Language, access security, appearance and maintenance
WELCOME_ADD_SHADE_DESC: Configure your RTS devices (Shades, gates, etc.)
WELCOME_FOOTER_NOTE: *Once a room, device, or group has been created, this page will no longer be displayed.
GENERAL_UPNP_DESC: Broadcasts service announcements on the network to allow automatic device discovery by other equipment
SECURITY_SECURE_CONFIG_DESC: Restricts settings changes to secure connections only to prevent unauthorized modifications
FW_UPDATE_UPTODATE: Your system is up to date
FW_UPDATE_ACTION_DESC2: Version %1 is ready to be installed
FW_UPDATE_USB_DESC: Version %1 requires a mandatory USB flash.
FW_UPDATE_FIRM_DESC: Local update of the system core (.bin)
FW_UPDATE_APP_DESC: Local update of the web interface only (.bin)
FW_CREDITS_BUG_DESC: Report a bug or a functional error
FW_CREDITS_SPC_DESC: A huge thank you to the original author for this project, without whom none of this would exist
CONNECTION_WIFI_DESC: Use the wireless connection via Wi-Fi network
CONNECTION_HIDDEN_SSID_DESC: Allows connection to a network that does not broadcast its name
CONNECTION_ROAMING_DESC: Always targets the nearest Wi-Fi access point
CONNECTION_ETHERNET_DESC: Uses Ethernet cable instead of Wi-Fi
CONNECTION_WIFI_FALLBACK_DESC: Switches to Wi-Fi if Ethernet fails
CONNECTION_AFTER_HOTSPOT_1: Once your settings are saved and after reboot, the access point
CONNECTION_ACCESS_METHODS_1: You can then access the interface via Hostname, e.g.:
ADRESSIP_IP_REBOOT_INFO: Settings will only be applied after restarting the ESPSomfy RTS module
MQTT_ENABLE_DESC: Enables communication with your MQTT server (broker)
MQTT_PUB_DISCOVERY_DESC: Sends settings for automatic configuration in your system
MQTT_ROOT_TOPIC_PLH: Prefix used for all messages from this module
MQTT_DISCOVERY_TOPIC_PLH: Path used for automatic discovery by your system, e.g.: homeassistant
FLIP_COMMAND_DESC: Reverses the direction of the commands
EMPTY_ROOM_TITLE: No custom rooms have been created
EMPTY_ROOM_DESC: Create rooms to organize your devices by zone (Living Room, Bedrooms, etc)
EMPTY_DEVICE_TITLE: No device has been configured yet
EMPTY_DEVICE_DESC: Add your first device to start monitoring and controlling it
EMPTY_GROUP_TITLE: No groups have been created yet
EMPTY_GROUP_DESC: Link your devices in a group to create centralized controls
EMPTY_REPEATER_TITLE: No remotes are currently being repeated
EMPTY_REPEATER_DESC: Use the button below to capture and duplicate a signal
ALL_DUPLICATE_REMOTES: All duplicate remote addresses are listed below.
DRAG_SORT: Drag each item to set the display order in the list.
NO_DEVICE: No devices are currently assigned to this room. You can add or move devices in the settings menu.
NAME: Name
GROUP_LINK_DESC: Starts the pairing process of a device to this group
RADIO_TOOLTIP_GPIO_1: The name (e.g., SCLK) refers to the pin on the CC1101 module.
RADIO_TOOLTIP_GPIO_2: The GPIO number (e.g., GPIO18) refers to the physical pin on your ESP32.
RADIO_TOOLTIP_GPIO_3: Refer to the 'pinout' diagram of your specific ESP32 model to locate them.
RADIO_TOOLTIP_GPIO_4: Note (excludes S2, S3, and C3 models): GPIO02 is reserved for the system (LED/Reset) and is therefore unavailable.
RADIO_BASE_FREQUENCY_HELP: Central communication frequency. Standard value: 433.42 MHz. Adjust in minimal increments if reception is poor. Return to 433.42 MHz if in doubt.
RADIO_RX_BANDWIDTH_HELP: Controls the width of the monitored spectrum. Narrower filters out more noise (better range). Wider makes the signal easier to capture.
RADIO_FREQ_DEVIATION_HELP: Determines the gap needed to distinguish data bits. High deviation requires a strong signal. Low deviation increases sensitivity but picks up more noise.
RADIO_TX_POWER_HELP: Transmission power. 10dBm is sufficient for most setups. Only use 12dBm if your hardware specifically supports it.
ETH_SETTINGS_DESC: Confirm your Ethernet settings before validating.
ETH_SETTINGS_WARNING_DESC_1: Incorrect Ethernet settings may damage your ESP32. Please verify the settings below and ensure they match the manufacturer specifications.
ETH_SETTINGS_WARNING_DESC_2: I confirm these settings are correct for my hardware. I understand that incorrect settings can permanently damage the ESP32
SCANFREQ_DESC: Scan surrounding frequencies to identify and configure your radio devices.
SCANFREQ_SCAN_DESC: Frequency scanning has started. Press and hold any button on your remote control and ESPSomfy RTS will find the closest frequency to the remote.
SCANFREQ_RSSI_EXPLANATION: RSSI measures radio signal strength. The closer the number is to 0 (e.g., -40), the stronger the signal. Closer to -100 means poor reception or interference.
SCANFREQ_RSSI_WEAK_DESC: Weak Signal: Risk of command loss
SCANFREQ_RSSI_NOISE_DESC: Noise: Unstable signal or out of range.
ROLLING_CODE_DESC: Warning: an incorrect value may break communication with the shade
ROLLING_CODE_WARNING_DESC_1: If this device is already paired with a motor, changing the rolling code will cause it to malfunction. Rolling codes are linked to the remote address, and the motor expects them to be sequential.
ROLLING_CODE_WARNING_DESC_2: If you hesitate even slightly, do not press the red button. Green represents safety — press it, wipe the sweat from your forehead, and follow the normal pairing process.
PAIR_GARAGE_DESC: Configuration wizard to guide you step-by-step through pairing your garage door with ESPSomfy-RTS.
UNPAIR_GARAGE_DESC: Configuration wizard to guide you step-by-step through unpairing your garage door from ESPSomfy-RTS.
PAIR_SHADE_DESC: Configuration wizard to guide you step-by-step through pairing your device with ESPSomfy-RTS.
UNPAIR_SHADE_DESC: Configuration wizard to guide you step-by-step through unpairing your device from ESPSomfy-RTS.
WIZ_GARAGE_STEP_1_1: Open your garage motor's memory (Refer to your manual to identify the exact procedure).
WIZ_GARAGE_STEP_1_2: Once the memory is open, don't rush; you usually have 2 minutes to act (please check your specific manual).
WIZ_GARAGE_STEP_1_3: You can now proceed to the next step by pressing the NEXT button.
WIZ_GARAGE_STEP_2_1: Press the PROG button above. ESPSomfy-RTS will send a Toggle command for single-button remotes, or a Prog signal for 3-button models.
WIZ_GARAGE_STEP_2_2: You can now press the NEXT button.
PAIR_GARAGE_STEP_3_1: Pairing is complete. Confirm by pressing the PAIRING SUCCESSFUL button above to close this wizard and save your garage door.
UNPAIR_GARAGE_STEP_3_1: Unpairing is complete. Confirm by pressing the UNPAIRING SUCCESSFUL button above to close this wizard and save your garage door.
PAIR_SHADE_STEP_1_1: Open the device's memory using a remote already paired with the motor: press the Prog button (usually on the back) until the device jogs briefly.
UNPAIR_SHADE_STEP_1_1: Open the device's memory using an existing remote: press the Prog button (usually on the back) until the device jogs briefly.
WIZ_SHADE_STEP_1_2: Programming mode is now active for 2 minutes. You have plenty of time to continue calmly. Press the NEXT button.
WIZ_SHADE_STEP_1_3: While 2 minutes is the RTS standard, feel free to check your model's manual if the motor seems to exit programming mode faster.
PAIR_SHADE_STEP_2_1: Now press the PROG button below to send the pairing signal to your device.
UNPAIR_SHADE_STEP_2_1: Now press the PROG button below to send the unpairing signal to your device.
WIZ_SHADE_STEP_2_2: It should jog again to confirm the registration. On some models, a long press may be necessary.
WIZ_SHADE_STEP_2_3: If the movement occurred, congratulations! Your device is ready. You can proceed to the final step by pressing the NEXT button.
WIZ_SHADE_STEP_2_4: If nothing happened, don't panic: the memory might still be open. Try pressing the PROG button above again.
PAIR_SHADE_STEP_3_1: Pairing is complete. Confirm by pressing the PAIRING SUCCESSFUL button above to close this wizard and save your device.
UNPAIR_SHADE_STEP_3_1: Unpairing is complete. Confirm by pressing the UNPAIRING SUCCESSFUL button above to close this wizard and save the changes.
LINK_REMOTE_DESC: Link your remote to the device
LINK_REMOTE_DESC_1: Press any button on the remote to link it to this device.
LINK_REMOTE_DESC_2: This will not change the remote's association, and this screen will close once the remote is detected.
REPEAT_REMOTE_DESC_1: Press any button on the remote control to repeat its signals.
REPEAT_REMOTE_DESC_2: Once assigned, ESPSomfy RTS will act as a repeater and repeat all signals from the identified remote controls.
REPEAT_REMOTE_DESC_3: Assign a repeater only when ESPSomfy RTS reliably receives a physical remote control but the motor does not.
REPEAT_REMOTE_DESC_4: Repeating unnecessary radio signals will degrade radio performance, and never assign the same repeater to more than one ESPSomfy RTS device. You would create an insidious echo chamber.
REPEAT_REMOTE_DESC_5: Once a signal is detected from the remote control, this window will close and the remote control signals will be repeated.
LINK_GROUP_DESC: Setup wizard to guide you step-by-step through adding your device to the group
LINK_GROUP_TITLE_STEP2: Selection of the device to be paired
LINK_GROUP_TITLE_STEP4: Pairing your device to the group
LINK_GROUP_STEP_1_1: This wizard will guide you through adding device to this group. Please follow each instruction carefully until the process is complete.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_1_2: During this procedure, the device must perform exactly two jog movements (brief up-and-down): the first to open the motor memory and the second to confirm the group addition.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_1_3: Each piece of device must be paired individually. When you are ready to begin, press the NEXT button.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_2_1: Choose the device you wish to include in this group using the selector above.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_2_2: Once the device is selected, press the NEXT button to continue.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_2_3: Only device not yet associated with this group is displayed. Note that a single device can belong to several different groups.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_3_1: Press the OPEN MEMORY button above. The selected device should perform a brief jog to confirm its memory is open.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_3_2: If the movement occurred, click YES in the confirmation window to proceed to the final step. Otherwise, click NO and try opening the memory again.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_3_3: Note: the motor should only perform one jog. If it performs several, the memory has been closed by mistake. A confirmation window will appear following your action.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_4_1: Press the PAIR TO GROUP button above to send the final association command.
LINK_GROUP_STEP_4_2: A confirmation window will appear. If the motor performed another jog, click YES. The wizard will then close automatically, and the association will be complete.
LINK_GROUP_MEMORY_READY_FOR_GROUP: Once the device jogs, the motor memory will be ready to add the device to the group.
LINK_GROUP_LINK_DONE: Once the device jogs, it will be added to the group and the process will be complete.
UNLINK_GROUP_DESC: Setup wizard to guide you step-by-step through removing your device from the group
UNLINK_GROUP_TITLE_STEP_3: Unpairing your device from the group
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_1_1: This wizard will guide you through removing this device from the group. Follow all instructions at each step until the device is removed from the group.
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_1_2: During this process, the device must perform exactly two jog movements. The first indicates the motor memory is open, and the second confirms the group has been deleted from the motor memory.
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_1_3: Each piece of device must be removed from the group individually. When you are ready to begin dissociating the device from the group, press the NEXT button to start.
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_2_1: You must first open the device's memory by pressing the OPEN MEMORY button. The device should perform a jog to indicate the memory is open.
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_2_2: If the movement occurred, click YES in the confirmation window to proceed to the final step. Otherwise, click NO and try opening the memory again.
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_2_3: The motor should only perform one jog. If it performs more than one, it means the motor memory has been closed.
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_3_1: Now you must send the group dissociation command; press the UNPAIR FROM GROUP button above.
UNLINK_GROUP_STEP_3_2: A confirmation window will appear. If the motor performed another jog, click YES. The wizard will then close automatically, and the dissociation will be complete.
UNLINK_GROUP_METHOD_1: If you have trouble getting the motor to react at this stage, you can try opening the memory using a remote control. This is usually done by selecting the channel and then holding the PROG button down.
UNLINK_GROUP_METHOD_2: If you have opened the memory using this alternative method, press the NO button to close this message, then the NEXT button to skip this step.
RESTORE_DESC: Recover your configurations and devices from a backup file.
RESTORE_SELECT_FILE: Select the options and your .backup file, then click the "UPLOAD FILE" button.
RESTORE_NETWORK_WARNING: Passwords (WiFi, MQTT) are not backed up and must be entered manually. Restoring to a new device will ignore Ethernet settings.
SAVESECURITY_PIN_WARNING: Please keep your PIN code in a safe place and do not forget it. The only way to recover a lost PIN code is to fully reload the initialization firmware, which will erase your entire configuration.
SAVESECURITY_PIN_CONFIRM: Have you saved your PIN code in a secure location?
SAVESECURITY_PASSWORD_WARNING: Please keep your password in a safe place and do not forget it. The only way to recover a lost password is to fully reload the initialization firmware, which will erase your entire configuration.
SAVESECURITY_PASSWORD_CONFIRM: Have you saved your username and password in a secure location?
GIT_RELEASE_DESC: Please wait while the version %1 is being downloaded and installed
GIT_RELEASE_WAIT_WARNING: Please wait while the files are being downloaded and installed.
GIT_RELEASE_WAIT_WARNING_1: Once the application update process has started, it can no longer be cancelled, otherwise the downloaded files may be corrupted.
GIT_RELEASE_SUCCES_2: Please wait a few moments and then reload the page.
UPDATE_GIT_DESC: Automated installation from available GitHub releases
UPDATE_GIT_RELEASE_BETA: You have selected a pre-released beta version that has not been fully tested or published for general use.
UPDATE_GIT_UPDATE_V3_BLOCKED: Upgrade to v3.0.0 is not possible due to a major partition table change. Manual USB flashing via the 'onboard' image is mandatory. Please refer to the documentation.
UPDATE_GIT_DOWNGRADE_V3_BLOCKED: Downgrade to a previous version is not possible. Your current partition table is not compatible with v2.x.x versions. Manual USB flashing is required to downgrade.
UPDATE_GIT_NOTE: Click directly on the link above.
FIRMWARE_UPDATE_FROM_GITHUB: If you don't have it locally: download from GitHub
FIRMWARE_UPDATE_VERIFY_0: Check that your backup is downloaded. If not, click the icon
FIRMWARE_CACHE: Remember to clear the cache after installation.
FIRMWARE_SAVE_BACKUP_DESC: A backup file will be downloaded to your browser
UPDATE_FIRMWARE_DESC: Upload the Firmware binary file to update the system.
UPDATE_LITTLEFS_DESC: Upload the LittleFS binary file to update system resources.

PROMPT_DELETE_GROUP: Are you sure you want to delete this group?
PROMPT_UNLINK_REPEATER: Are you sure you want to stop repeating frames from this address?
PROMPT_UNLINK_REMOTE: Are you sure you want to unlink this remote from the device?
PROMPT_SHADE_MOVE_CONFIRM: Did the device jog? If so, press YES, otherwise press NO and try again.
PROMPT_SHADE_MOVE_DONE: Once the device jogs, it will be removed from the group and the process will be complete.
PROMPT_SHADE_OPEN_MEMORY_CONFIRM: Did the device jog? If so, press YES, otherwise press NO and try again.
PROMPT_SHADE_GROUP_LINK_CONFIRM: Did the device jog? If so, press YES and your device will be linked to the group. Otherwise, press NO and try again.
PROMPT_DELETE_ROOM_WARNING: If this room was previously selected for motors or groups, they will be automatically assigned to the "Home" room.
PROMPT_DELETE_SHADE_WARNING: If this device was previously paired with a motor, you should unpair it from the motor and remove it from all groups first. Otherwise, its address will remain in the motor's memory.
PROMPT_DELETE_SHADE_CONFIRM: Press YES to delete {SHADE_NAME}, or NO to cancel this operation.
PROMPT_DELETE_GROUP_CONFIRM: Press YES to delete the group {GROUP_NAME}, or NO to cancel this operation.
PROMPT_REBOOT_CONFIRM: Are you sure you want to reboot the device?
PROMPT_RESTORE_FILE_DESC: This file is from a version prior to v2.5.0. Radio GPIO pin settings will not be restored correctly.
PROMPT_RESTORE_FILE_DESC_1: Please manually verify your configuration in the Radio tab after importing.
PROMPT_RESTORE_FILE_DESC_2: Do you want to continue with this file?
BT_LINK_REMOTE_DESC: Link a physical remote or sensor

ERR_HOSTNAME_CHARS: The host name must only include numbers, letters, or dash.
ERR_HOSTNAME_LENGTH: The maximum Host Name length is 32 characters.
ERR_NTP_LENGTH: The maximum NTP Server length is 64 characters.
ERR_PIN_INVALID_DESC: The PIN code must contain exactly 4 alphanumeric characters. Please enter a complete code.
ERR_USERNAME_MISSING_DESC: You must provide a username for password security.
ERR_USERNAME_INVALID_DESC: The maximum length of the username is 32 characters.
ERR_PASSWORD_MISSING_DESC: You must provide a password for password security.
ERR_PASSWORD_INVALID_DESC: The maximum length of the password is 32 characters.
ERR_PASSWORD_CONFIRM_MISSING_DESC: You must re-enter the password in the "Re-enter password" field.
ERR_PASSWORD_MISMATCH_DESC: Please re-enter the password exactly as entered in the confirmation field.
ERR_STATIC_IP_REQUIRED: You must provide a valid IP address for the static IP.
ERR_STATIC_IP_INVALID: Invalid static IP address. Expected format is XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX.
ERR_NETMASK_REQUIRED: You must provide a valid IP address for the subnet mask.
ERR_NETMASK_INVALID: Invalid subnet mask IP address. Expected format is XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX.
ERR_GATEWAY_REQUIRED: You must provide a valid IP address for the gateway.
ERR_GATEWAY_INVALID: Invalid gateway IP address. Expected format is XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX.
ERR_DNS1_INVALID: Invalid DNS server 1 IP address. Expected format is XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX.
ERR_DNS2_INVALID: Invalid DNS server 2 IP address. Expected format is XXX.XXX.XXX.XXX.
ERR_WIFI_SSID_MAX_LENGTH_64: The maximum length of the SSID is 64 characters.
ERR_WIFI_PASSPHRASE_MAX_LENGTH_64: The maximum length of the passphrase is 64 characters.
ERR_RADIO_TYPE_REQUIRED: You must select a radio type.
ERR_RADIO_PINS_REQUIRED: You must define all the pins for the radio.
ERR_GPIO_PIN_DUPLICATED: The %1 pin is duplicated by the %2.  All pin definitions must be unique
ERR_ROOM_NAME_INVALID: You must provide a name for the room between 1 and 20 characters.
ERR_ROOM_LIMIT_REACHED: Maximum room limit reached. Please delete one before creating a new one.
ERR_DEVICE_LIMIT_REACHED: Maximum device limit reached. Please delete one before creating a new one.
ERR_GROUP_LIMIT_REACHED: Maximum group limit reached. Please delete one before creating a new one.
ERR_REMOTE_ADDRESS_INVALID: The remote address must be a number between 1 and 16,777,215. This number must be unique for all devices.
ERR_DEVIVE_NAME_INVALID: You must provide a device name between 1 and 20 characters.
ERR_UP_TIME_INVALID: Up time must be between 0 and 180,000 milliseconds (3 minutes). It corresponds to the travel time of the device from fully closed to fully open.
ERR_DOWN_TIME_INVALID: Down time must be between 0 and 180,000 milliseconds (3 minutes). It corresponds to the travel time of the device from fully open to fully closed.
ERR_GPIO_UP_DOWN_NOT_UNIQUE: For GPIO controlled motors the up and down GPIO selections must be unique.
ERR_GPIO_UP_DOWN_MY_NOT_UNIQUE: For GPIO controlled motors the up and down and my GPIO selections must be unique.
ERR_ROOM_ID_REQUIRED: A valid room id was not supplied.
ERR_SHADE_ID_REQUIRED: Device ID is missing or invalid.
ERR_DEVICE_IN_GROUP: Cannot delete this device because it belongs to a group.
ERR_GROUP_NOT_EMPTY: You cannot delete this group until all devices have been removed from it.
ERR_INVALID_GROUP_ID: A valid group ID was not provided
ERR_NO_SHADE_AVAILABLE_FOR_GROUP: No devices available to associate with this group.
ERR_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND_GROUP: The specified device was not found in this group.
ERR_UNSPECIFIED: Missing file or version unavailable for your ESP32
ERR_MQTT_HOSTNAME_REQUIRED: You must supply a host name to connect to MQTT.
ERR_HOSTNAME_MAX_LENGTH_64: The maximum length of the host name is 64 characters.
ERR_MQTT_PORT_HINT: Likely ports are 1183, 8883 for MQTT/S or 80,443 for HTTP/S
ERR_USERNAME_MAX_LENGTH_32: The maximum length of the username is 32 characters.
ERR_PASSWORD_MAX_LENGTH_32: The maximum length of the password is 32 characters.
ERR_ROOT_TOPIC_MAX_LENGTH_64: The maximum length of the root topic is 64 characters.

ERR_INVALID_FILE_LITTLEFS: This file is not a valid littleFS binary file.
ERR_NO_FILE_FIRMWARE_SELECTED: You must select a valid firmware binary file to proceed.
ERR_INVALID_FILE_FIRMWARE: This file is not a valid firmware binary file.
